common.unauthorizedBody: You need to sign in to continue.

auth.loginTitle: Sign in to your rider cockpit
auth.loginBody: Track shifts, profitability, and operating costs in one place.
auth.registerBody: Set up a bilingual analytics workspace for every shift you work.

dashboard.title: Know whether your shifts are actually profitable
dashboard.body: Track real revenue, true costs, and operational rhythm with a dashboard built for a working courier.

quickRead.title: Quick read
quickRead.body: See the essentials first: pace, margin, movement, and the next useful action.
quickRead.paceBody: {value} average revenue per shift in this period.
quickRead.deeperBody: Open the deeper view only when you want to inspect patterns, weather, and composition in detail.

empty.title: No shifts yet
empty.body: Start with your first shift to unlock profitability tracking and trend analysis.

setup.vehicleBody: Define vehicle assumptions, recurring categories, and manual expenses so your net profit reflects real life.
setup.costBody: Build your own business and personal budget categories and keep fixed costs honest.

dashboard.quickActionsBody: Keep the daily tools close: add spending fast or jump back into your operating flow.

quickExpense.launchBody: Log fuel, snacks, parking, or a quick cost without leaving the dashboard.
quickExpense.panelBody: Add a cost in a few taps using the categories you already set up.
quickExpense.modalBody: Pick one of your saved categories, enter the amount, and keep moving.
quickExpense.fetchError: We couldn't load your expense categories.
quickExpense.emptyCategories: Set up at least one expense category in Settings first.

dashboard.settingsCardBody: Open vehicle assumptions, budgets, security, and the rest of your RiderMeter settings.

weather.title: Profitability by weather
weather.body: See how different weather conditions change your net per hour and the shape of each shift.
weather.emptyBody: Log a few shifts with weather selected to unlock weather-aware profitability.

benchmarks.title: Network benchmarks
benchmarks.body: Anonymous rider aggregates show where the network tends to be strongest.
benchmarks.emptyBody: Benchmark cards appear automatically once the anonymous network sample is large enough.

insights.emptyTitle: Your next useful insight is loading
insights.emptyBody: Add a few more clean shifts and RiderMeter will start surfacing stronger patterns.

charts.trendBody: Follow the shape of your period and spot expensive days early.
charts.weekdayBody: See which weekdays return the strongest net profit per hour.
charts.revenueCompositionBody: Understand how much of your income comes from base pay, tips, and bonuses.
charts.costCompositionBody: See what is consuming your margin.

bestWeekday.title: Strongest weekday
bestWeekday.body: {weekday} is currently your best weekday at {value} net per hour.

weekUp.title: Weekly pace improved
weekUp.body: Net profit per hour is up by {delta}% versus the previous week.

weekDown.title: Weekly pace slowed
weekDown.body: Net profit per hour is down by {delta}% versus the previous week.

highKm.title: Long-distance shifts need attention
highKm.body: High-kilometer shifts are returning only {margin}% margin in this period.

tips.title: Tips matter
tips.body: Tips represent {share}% of your revenue in this period.

weather.title: Weather is changing the outcome
weather.body: {weather} currently returns {value} net per hour for you.

bestWeekday.title: Best weekday across riders
bestWeekday.body: Across the network, {weekday} is currently strongest at {revenueValue} revenue per hour.

bestWindow.title: Strongest time window
bestWindow.body: The network is strongest around {window}, with {revenueValue} revenue per hour.

revenuePerHour.title: Network revenue benchmark
revenuePerHour.body: Anonymous rider data is averaging {revenueValue} revenue per hour.

ordersPerHour.title: Network order benchmark
ordersPerHour.body: Anonymous rider data is averaging {ordersValue} orders per hour.

shiftForm.title: Capture a shift without slowing down
shiftForm.body: Choose a fast entry style, capture weather, and save a complete shift from your phone without friction.
shiftForm.finishTitle: Finish a shift in one quick pass
shiftForm.finishBody: Enter only the six essentials first, save fast, and keep the extra details tucked away unless you need them.
shiftForm.quickTitle: Quick entry for after the shift
shiftForm.finishEssentialsBody: These are the only values you usually need right after a shift ends.
shiftForm.moreDetailsBody: Fuel overrides, tolls, and notes can stay tucked away unless this shift really needs them.
shiftForm.quickHelp: Use this when you know the total duration and want the fastest possible entry.
shiftForm.prefilledToday: Last shift setup loaded for today.
shiftForm.readyBody: Start with the essentials below and save fast. You can always add the extra details after.
shiftForm.mobileDockHint: Add the essentials and save when ready.

finishStrip.title: Six taps and done
finishStrip.body: A fast finish usually needs duration, activity, and payout only.

quickActions.body: Tap the common values first, then fine-tune only what needs it.

shortcuts.title: Use your last rhythm
shortcuts.body: Pull in only the parts of the previous shift that still fit today.

help.fuelExpenseDirect: Use only when you want to override estimated fuel cost.
help.notes: Weather, demand, delays, or anything worth remembering.

duration.title: Working duration
duration.manualHelp: Use this only if you do not want to save start and end times.

errors.generic: Something went wrong while saving the shift.
errors.invalid: Please correct the highlighted fields before saving.
errors.hoursWorked: Hours worked must be greater than zero.
errors.nonNegative: Value must be zero or greater.

history.title: Review every shift with less noise
history.body: Filter by date and platform, then compare revenue, costs, and shift notes cleanly.
history.emptyBody: Try a wider date range or create your first shift.

admin.title: Privacy-safe admin workspace
admin.body: Review adoption, activity, and billing state without exposing rider emails or personal operational notes.
admin.forbiddenBody: This area is only available to admin accounts.

users.title: Accounts and billing state
users.body: Each account is shown with an internal public code and operational metrics only.

settings.vehicleBody: Configure cost-per-kilometer assumptions once and reuse them everywhere.
settings.costBody: Capture recurring operating costs so net profit stays honest.

setupWorkspace.title: Turn your rider profile into a real profit model
setupWorkspace.body: Pick your vehicle, define cost assumptions from real maintenance intervals, and build your own business and personal expense categories.
setupWorkspace.settingsTitle: Settings that keep your numbers honest
setupWorkspace.settingsBody: Update vehicle assumptions, budgets, manual expenses, and account security any time from one place.

sections.vehicleBody: Choose car, scooter, or e-bike, including electric car or scooter setups, and enter the maintenance rules you already know. RiderMeter turns them into cost-per-kilometer automatically.
sections.budgetBody: Create your own expense categories such as insurance, mobile, snacks, gas, or anything else you want to track regularly.
sections.expensesBody: Add one-off or day-to-day expenses whenever they happen and keep a recent ledger close by.
sections.recentBody: Review the latest manual expenses you logged from your setup workspace.

messages.onboardingSaved: Startup guide completed. Taking you to your dashboard.
messages.error: Failed to save the setup workspace.

account.title: Password and account access
account.body: Keep your RiderMeter account secure and update your password whenever you need to.

typeBodies.car: Best when you need full operating-cost visibility for a car-based delivery setup.
typeBodies.scooter: Ideal for city delivery with fuel, service, and tire assumptions.
typeBodies.ebike: Built for electric delivery setups where charging, service, and lifecycle matter most.

vehicle.fuelConsumptionHelp: Example: 3.2 liters / 100 km for a scooter or 7.5 for a car.
vehicle.fuelPriceHelp: Use the price you usually pay, not the theoretical best case.
vehicle.ebikeConsumptionHelp: Use the charging cost equivalent for 100 km.
vehicle.ebikePriceHelp: Set the cost level you want RiderMeter to use for charging.

categories.name: Category name
categories.scopeHelp: Decide whether this affects business costs or your personal budget.

expenses.unsavedCategory: Save your categories first, then log expenses against them.
expenses.empty: No expense entries yet. Add your first real-world cost here.
